rationale: The page contains multiple distinct technological updates including RBI's CBDC pilot projects, Bluetooth Mesh Networking (Bitchat), Meta's Project Waterworth undersea cable, and ISRO's Vikram3201 processor. These provide several factual points suitable for current affairs questions.
cleanedText: The RBI is currently conducting pilot projects of two types of CBDCs – retail and wholesale. Retail CBDC: It is available to the general public via digital wallets, smartphone apps, etc. Wholesale CBDCs: Used among banks and other licensed financial institutions for interbank payments and securities transactions. 2.6. OTHER IMPORTANT NEWS 2.6.1. BLUETOOTH MESH NETWORKING Twitter Co-Founder shared details of Bluetooth Messaging App, Bitchat. Bitchat relies on Bluetooth Low Energy Mesh Networking to enable communication. What is Bluetooth Mesh Networking? Relies on Bluetooth clusters or Mesh network created by devices in a range. Mesh network, also known as ‘multi-hop network’, is a networking topology. In it, data can travel from any device to all others, achieving many-to-many communication. Even if one device fails, the network keeps working. Messages are broadcast and relayed by nodes until they reach the intended destination. Potential Benefits: No central database: Messages are stored entirely on users’ devices and are deleted after a short period. This design is created to priorities users’ privacy. Also, Messages are end-to-end encrypted and do not leave the network. No registration required: Users are not required to create accounts using their email or phone number, making the communication anonymous. 2.6.2. PROJECT WATERWORTH Meta has selected Mumbai and Visakhapatnam as the landing sites for the undersea cable project, Waterworth. It will connect five major continents. It is the world’s longest subsea cable project. About Undersea Cables: Undersea cables are fiber-optic cable laid across the ocean floor that transmits information and enables worldwide communications. Significance: Undersea cables are backbone of international internet. 2.6.3. VIKRAM3201 India’s first 32-bit processor – Vikram3201 – presented to the PM at Semicon India 2025. About Vikram3201 Processor: An advanced version of the indigenously designed 16-bit VIKRAM1601 microprocessor which has been flying in the Avionics system of ISRO’s launch vehicles. Fully ‘Make-in-India’ 32-bit microprocessor qualified for the harsh environmental conditions of launch vehicles. Designed and developed by the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre of ISRO in collaboration with the Semiconductor Laboratory (SCL) in Chandigarh. They have also developed KALPANA3201, another 32-bit microprocessor. 2.6.4. FASTAG Ministry of Road Transport and Highways launched FASTag Annual Pass facility.
